questions: Python tools (place.py, view.py, common.py) are outside the repo and I couldn't read them. Can you copy them into the repo (e.g., investigation/ or a temp folder) so I can port the exact logic? Or should I implement the tools from scratch based on the type definitions?

For the chat UI placement — should AgentChat replace the PropertiesPanel when open (toggle between them), sit as a resizable side panel alongside it, or be a floating overlay/drawer? The shell is already dense with 3 panels.

Should the agent be able to create generation tasks (e.g., generate new video clips via the existing task system)? The tool list includes create_generation_task but this requires knowing which task types are appropriate and having project context. Should this be deferred to a later phase?

For the auto-continuation loop: when the agent sets status=continue, should the client re-invoke immediately (200ms delay), or should there be a visible 'Agent is thinking...' state with the option for the user to intervene before it continues?
success_criteria: New DB migration adds config_version to timelines and creates timeline_agent_sessions table with RLS and realtime
Shared transcription module extracted from ai-voice-prompt without breaking existing functionality
ai-timeline-agent edge function accepts session_id + user_message, runs tool-use loop, and writes updated config back to timelines table with optimistic locking
All 10 tools (view, move, trim, set_property, delete, add_text, find_issues, create_task, message_user, done) implemented and callable by the LLM
Kill switch works: setting session status to cancelled causes the agent to stop processing within one turn
Client chat UI shows conversation turns, supports text and voice input, and auto-continues on status=continue
Timeline updates from the agent appear in real-time in the editor (via existing realtime subscription)
Agent can handle a multi-step request like 'close the gap between clips 3 and 4' end-to-end in under 5 seconds
assumptions: Groq API supports tool-use / function calling in chat completions (Groq added tool-use support for most models in 2024)
Edge function timeout is ~60s (Supabase default for Pro plan), which is enough for ~8 LLM turns with Groq's fast inference
The Python tools (place.py, view.py, common.py) implement straightforward clip manipulation logic that can be reimplemented from the TimelineConfig type definitions without needing exact source
Multiple concurrent agent sessions on the same timeline are safe because each session operates on a fresh config read and uses versioned writes — conflicts are detected and handled
The existing realtime subscription on the timelines table will fire when the edge function (using admin client) updates config, so no additional realtime wiring is needed for live updates
Voice transcription latency via Groq Whisper is ~500ms for typical utterances, meeting the parallel-load performance target
The session turns JSONB column is sufficient for conversation history — no need for a separate turns table unless sessions grow very large (>100 turns)
